'''Brett Ryan Ferres''' (born 17 April 1986) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[Betfred Championship]], and has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level. He played initially as a {{rlp|ce}} and later as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

He has previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[http://www.giantsrl.com/locker_room/first_team/player?brett-ferres Huddersfield Giants Profile]</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/profiles.cgi?11|title=Brett Ferres Bio|work=Cas Tigers Official Website|date=2 July 2009|access-date=2 July 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100105034431/http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/profiles.cgi?11|archive-date=5 January 2010|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==Background==
Ferres was born in [[Castleford]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, and was educated at [[Castleford Academy]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Schools RL: Secret of Castleford Academy's success is 'in the blood' |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/schools-rl-secret-of-castleford-academys-success-is-in-the-blood-1770718 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=25 August 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Ferres was a member of the England academy side that toured Australia and New Zealand in 2004. In 2005 he made 9 first team appearances for [[Bradford Bulls]], and was the regular goal kicker for the senior academy, a side he also captained, scoring 17 tries in only 17 appearances for the under-21s.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://archive.thisisbradford.co.uk/2004/8/7/98363.html|title=Fans Make a World of Difference|work=This Is Bradford|date=7 August 2004|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref>
As [[Super League]] champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]], Ferres played from the interchange bench in Bradford's 30–10 victory.

[[File:Brett Ferres.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Ferres playing for the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
In late 2006, Ferres signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in exchange for [[David Solomona]] who went to the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] club.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.metro.co.uk/sport/rugby/article.html?in_article_id=24529&in_page_id=45|title=Ferres signs for Wildcats|work=Metro.co.uk|date=9 November 2006|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
Ferres signed for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on 7 November 2008 after being released from his contract with Wakefield Trinity.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/7716717.stm|title=Tigers sign young forward Ferres|work=[[BBC]]|date=7 November 2008|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref>

On 2 July 2009 Ferres signed a new three-year deal to stay at Castleford.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://castigers.com/cgi-bin/news.cgi?722_30319|title=New deal for Brett|work=Cas Tigers Official Website|date=2 July 2009|access-date=2 July 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090704221618/http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/news.cgi?722_30319|archive-date=4 July 2009|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
In July 2012, Ferres signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] for a three-and-a-half-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18928141|title=Huddersfield Giants sign Brett Ferres from Castleford Tigers|work=BBC Sport |access-date=2012-07-20}}</ref> He was named at second-row in the [[Super League Dream Team#2013 Dream Team|2013 Super League Dream Team]]. Immediately following the test series against New Zealand, Ferres was suspended by Huddersfield pending an investigation into his "conduct away from the club".<ref>{{cite web |title=England forward Brett Ferres suspended by Huddersfield pending investigation |date=2015-11-18 |website=[[The Guardian]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220815145909/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/nov/18/england-forward-brett-ferres-suspended-huddersfield |archive-date=2022-08-15 |url-status=live |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/nov/18/england-forward-brett-ferres-suspended-huddersfield}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
In January 2016, [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] signed Brett Ferres from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] for an undisclosed fee.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/jan/04/brett-ferres-signs-leeds-rhinos-huddersfield-giants-rugby-league|title=Leeds Rhinos secure signing of Brett Ferres from Huddersfield Giants|work=[[The Guardian]]|date=4 January 2016|access-date=5 January 2015}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Castleford at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6–24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|work=The Guardian|date=7 October 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6–24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=The Guardian|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
Between 2020-2022, Ferres played for Featherstone in the RFL Championship. In December 2022, it was announced that he would be departing the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-leeds-rhinos-featherstone-rovers-and-bradford-bulls-man-brett-ferres-finds-new-club/|title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos, Featherstone Rovers and Bradford Bulls man Brett Ferres finds new club|website=www.totalrl.com|date=26 December 2022 }}</ref>

===Doncaster===
In 2023, Ferres joined Doncaster. On 15 November 2023, Ferres signed a one-year contract extension to remain at the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-champion-brett-ferres-signs-up-to-play-20th-season-at-the-age-of-37-doncaster/|title=Former Super League champion signs up to play 20th season at age of 37: 'Really excited to continue the rugby league rollercoaster'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=14 November 2023 }}</ref>

===Goole Vikings===
On 16 Oct 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=16 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Super League and Championship forward Brett Ferres joins shock club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-super-league-and-championship-forward-brett-ferres-joins-shock-club/}}</ref>

==International career==
Ferres won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2006 against France, Tonga (2 matches) and Samoa in the [[2006 Federation Shield]] tournament.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6059844.stm|title=England 26–10 France|work=[[BBC]]|date=22 October 2006|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6091444.stm|title=England 40–18 Tonga|work=[[BBC]]|date=29 October 2006|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6103128.stm|title=England 38–14 Samoa|work=[[BBC]]|date=5 November 2006|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6130068.stm|title=England 31–14 Tonga|work=[[BBC]]|date=12 November 2006|access-date=2 July 2009}}</ref>

In 2013, Ferres was named in the [[England national rugby league team|English squad]] for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. Ferres was selected after [[Gareth Hock]] breached team policy and was removed from the squad. Ferres featured in every game England played.

In 2014, Ferres played for England in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] held in Australia and New Zealand.

In 2015, Ferres was selected in the England 24-man squad to take on New Zealand in a [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test-series]]. Before the series began England played a match against [[France national rugby league team|France]]. Brett scored 3 of the 15 tries scored by England in the match in what was a demolishing of their opponents.

In 2016, Ferres was initially selected in the England 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]], but had to withdraw due to a knee injury.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Stevie Ward called into England squad for injured Brett Ferres
|publisher = skySports
|date = 18 October 2016
|url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10622486/stevie-ward-called-into-england-squad-for-injured-brett-ferres
|access-date = 18 October 2016}}</ref>
